statement: LLM-guided self-debugging follows exponential decay — each successive attempt at the same bug is less likely to succeed. If 2-3 fix attempts fail, reset context entirely and try a completely different approach rather than letting the agent keep looping with minor variations.

evidenceRefs.title: Measuring and mitigating debugging effectiveness decay in code — Nature (2025)

publicSummary: Debugging effectiveness decays exponentially per attempt. Reset context after 2-3 failed fixes.
cautionNotes: The Nature paper measured LLM self-debugging specifically; human debugging may not follow the same curve.
